name: Machine Shoulder Press
resistance_profile: The machine's cam and lever geometry keep tension fairly even from shoulder level to near lockout, smoothing out the sticking point a free-weight press has just off the shoulders.
cues: Press up smoothly, don't lock hard
execution_steps: Adjust the seat so the handles sit at about shoulder height.
Sit back with your torso against the pad and grip the handles.
Press up smoothly until your arms are nearly extended, without slamming into a hard lockout.
Lower under control back to shoulder level.
good_when: You are a beginner or want a guided, low-skill overhead press.
You want to train close to failure safely without a spotter.
You are finishing shoulders after heavier free-weight pressing.
bad_when: You want to train stabilizers and real-world overhead control.
The machine's fixed path does not fit your limb lengths comfortably.
You want a deep stretch under load (dumbbells go deeper).
